meta.attribution: Brodaric & Richard 2021, GSC Open File 8796, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:augen_lineation.definition_en_canonical: A shape lineation defined by elongate augen.
gso:augen_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:bedding_cleavage_intersection_lineat.definition_en_canonical: Lineation defined by the intersection of bedding surfaces and cleavage surfaces.
gso:bedding_cleavage_intersection_lineat.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:biotite_mineral_lineation.definition_en_canonical: Mineral lineation defined by aligned elongate biotite aggregates in a foliation surface. Biotite does not have an elongate (prismatic, acicular) mineral habit, so production of lineation must reflect elongate aggregates of biotite crystals.
gso:biotite_mineral_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:cleavage_intersection_lineation.definition_en_canonical: Lineation defined by the intersection of two cleavages.
gso:cleavage_intersection_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:crenulation_lineation.definition_en_canonical: Lineation defined by small-scale wrinkles, typically of phylosillicate minerals, on another surface, typically a cleavage or schistosity. Use instead of intersection lineation if the wrinkles are the measured feature and the intersecting planar feature is not apparent.
gso:crenulation_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:flow_lineation.definition_en_canonical: A linear alignment of fabric elements, especially elongate or rod-shaped minerals, interpreted to have been aligned by flow of magma, lava, pyroclastic material, or diapiric flow of sedimentary rock.
gso:flow_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:hornblende_mineral_lineation.definition_en_canonical: Mineral lineation defined by aligned hornblende crystals.
gso:hornblende_mineral_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:igneous_lineation.definition_en_canonical: Lineation defined by alignment of elongate fabric elements formed in lava or magma before complete crystallization. Some typical fabric elements are elongate phenocrysts and vesicles.
gso:igneous_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:igneous_mineral_lineation.definition_en_canonical: Lineation in igneous rock defined by aligned crystals where there is evidence that the crystal alignment is related to process occurring before the rock was solidified.
gso:igneous_mineral_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:igneous_particle_shape_lineation.definition_en_canonical: Lineation in an igneous rock, usually welded tuff, defined by elongate objects in the rock (usually pumice pads, also elongate crystals). Descriptive--elogation may be due to deformation or to rotation/alignment of originally elongate (prolate ellipsoid) objects in the rock.
gso:igneous_particle_shape_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:igneous_vesicle_lineation.definition_en_canonical: lineation defined in a volcanic rock by aligned elongation direction of vesicles.
gso:igneous_vesicle_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:intersection_lineation.definition_en_canonical: A lineation produced by the intersection of two planar fabrics.
sources: Neuendorf et al 2005, CGI lineationtype SKOS vocabulary 2016-11-21
gso:intersection_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:mineral_lineation.definition_en_canonical: Lineation defined by alignment of elongate crystals that have a prismatic crystal habit or of monomineralic grain aggregates.
sources: Neuendorf et al 2005, CGI lineationtype SKOS vocabulary 2016-11-21
gso:mineral_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:particle_shape_lineation.definition_en_canonical: Lineation defined by alignment of long axes of prolate-shaped particles such as clasts, fossils, crystals, or phenocrysts. Particles may be polycrystalline aggregates formed by deformation of pre-existing particles. General category does not specify genesis of particles or their alignment.
gso:particle_shape_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:pencil_lineation.definition_en_canonical: Lineation defined by tendency of rock to break into long, slender pieces resembling pencils. Typically produced by intesection of bedding and cleavage or intersection of two cleavages. Fabric is generally only observed in weakly metamorphosed rocks.
gso:pencil_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:quartz_fibre_lineation.definition_en_canonical: Aligned quartz fibres that define a measurable lineation
gso:quartz_fibre_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:rodding_lineation.definition_en_canonical: Linear structure in metamorphic rocks in which the stronger parts have been shaped into parallel rods (eg. vein quartz)
gso:rodding_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:sillimanite_mineral_lineation.definition_en_canonical: Mineral lineation defined by aligned sillimanite crystals.
gso:sillimanite_mineral_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:slickenline.definition_en_canonical: Lineation on a brittle slip surface defined by grooves, ridges, or striations
gso:slickenline.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:stretching_lineation.definition_en_canonical: Lineation defined by elongate (prolate) fabric elements in which the alignment of the fabric elements, and the formation of prolate fabric elements is interpreted to be the product of crystal plastic deformation processes. Associated with mylontic foliation.
gso:stretching_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296

gso:tectonic_lineation.definition_en_canonical: Penetrative linear structure in a rock body defined by fabric elements that are the product of tectonic processes. Linear is used in the sense that the orientation of the structure can be represented as a line in some sufficiently small neighborhood of any point in the material containig the fabric. Penetrative denotes that the fabric elements defining the structure are repeated at distances so small, compared with the scale of the whole that they can be considered to pervade it uniformly and be present at every point (paraphrase of Turner and Weiss, 1963, p. 21).
gso:tectonic_lineation.attribution: GSO v1.0 (Brodaric & Richard 2021), GSC OF 8796, CC BY 4.0, DOI 10.4095/328296
